title: Kingdom of Kottayam

[newspapers](https://www.google.com/search?&q=%22Kingdom+of+Kottayam%22&tbs=bkt:s&tbm=bks) **·** [books](https://www.google.com/search?tbs=bks:1&q=%22Kingdom+of+Kottayam%22+-wikipedia) **·** [scholar](https://scholar.google.com/scholar?q=%22Kingdom+of+Kottayam%22) **·** [JSTOR](https://www.jstor.org/action/doBasicSearch?Query=%22Kingdom+of+Kottayam%22&acc=on&wc=on) *(December 2020)* *([Learn how and when to remove this message](/wiki/Help:Maintenance_template_removal "Help:Maintenance template removal"))* |

was a feudal city-state in [Malabar](/wiki/Malabar_Coast "Malabar Coast"), present-day [Kerala](/wiki/Kerala "Kerala"), India.[[1]](#cite_note-Logan2000-1) Kottayam (Cotiote) is famed for [Pazhassi Raja](/wiki/Pazhassi_Raja "Pazhassi Raja"), one of the principal leaders of the Wayanad Insurrection (Kottayam Pazhassi rebellion or [Kottayam War](/wiki/Cotiote_War "Cotiote War")). Pazhassi Raja was a member of the western branch of the Kottayam royal clan.[[2]](#cite_note-:0-2) When [Hyder Ali](/wiki/Hyder_Ali "Hyder Ali") of the [Kingdom of Mysore](/wiki/Kingdom_of_Mysore "Kingdom of Mysore") [occupied Malabar](/wiki/Mysore_invasion_of_Kerala "Mysore invasion of Kerala") in 1773, the Raja of Kottayam found political asylum in [Travancore](/wiki/Travancore "Travancore"). In 1790, the British recognized Pazhassi Raja as the head of Kottayam instead of the original Raja who had taken refuge at Travancore.[[3]](#cite_note-3)

William Logan (2000). [*Malabar Manual*](https://books.google.com/books?id=9mR2QXrVEJIC&pg=PA1). Asian Educational Services. pp. 1–. [ISBN](/wiki/ISBN_(identifier) "ISBN (identifier)") [978-81-206-0446-9](/wiki/Special:BookSources/978-81-206-0446-9 "Special:BookSources/978-81-206-0446-9").

[Coordinates](/wiki/Geographic_coordinate_system "Geographic coordinate system"): [11°49′41″N 75°33′00″E﻿ / ﻿11.828°N 75.55°E﻿ / 11.828; 75.55](https://geohack.toolforge.org/geohack.php?pagename=Kingdom_of_Kottayam&params=11.828_N_75.55_E_)

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Feudal city-state in Malabar, India

For the city at the centre of the kingdom, see [Kottayam-Malabar](/wiki/Kottayam-Malabar "Kottayam-Malabar").

Not to be confused with [Kottayam](/wiki/Kottayam "Kottayam").

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [icon](/wiki/File:Question_book-new.svg) | This article **needs additional citations for [verification](/wiki/Wikipedia:Verifiability "Wikipedia:Verifiability")**. Please help [improve this article](/wiki/Special:EditPage/Kingdom_of_Kottayam "Special:EditPage/Kingdom of Kottayam") by [adding citations to reliable sources](/wiki/Help:Referencing_for_beginners "Help:Referencing for beginners"). Unsourced material may be challenged and removed. *Find sources:* ["Kingdom of Kottayam"](https://www.google.com/search?as_eq=wikipedia&q=%22Kingdom+of+Kottayam%22) – [news](https://www.google.com/search?tbm=nws&q=%22Kingdom+of+Kottayam%22+-wikipedia&tbs=ar:1) **·** [newspapers](https://www.google.com/search?&q=%22Kingdom+of+Kottayam%22&tbs=bkt:s&tbm=bks) **·** [books](https://www.google.com/search?tbs=bks:1&q=%22Kingdom+of+Kottayam%22+-wikipedia) **·** [scholar](https://scholar.google.com/scholar?q=%22Kingdom+of+Kottayam%22) **·** [JSTOR](https://www.jstor.org/action/doBasicSearch?Query=%22Kingdom+of+Kottayam%22&acc=on&wc=on) *(December 2020)* *([Learn how and when to remove this message](/wiki/Help:Maintenance_template_removal "Help:Maintenance template removal"))* |

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/1/14/Veera_Kerala_Varma_Pazhassi_Raja.jpg/250px-Veera_Kerala_Varma_Pazhassi_Raja.jpg)](/wiki/File:Veera_Kerala_Varma_Pazhassi_Raja.jpg)

Veera Kerala Varma [Pazhassi Raja](/wiki/Pazhassi_Raja "Pazhassi Raja"); painting by [Raja Ravi Varma](/wiki/Raja_Ravi_Varma "Raja Ravi Varma").

**Kottayam (Cotiote)** was a feudal city-state in [Malabar](/wiki/Malabar_Coast "Malabar Coast"), present-day [Kerala](/wiki/Kerala "Kerala"), India.[[1]](#cite_note-Logan2000-1) Kottayam (Cotiote) is famed for [Pazhassi Raja](/wiki/Pazhassi_Raja "Pazhassi Raja"), one of the principal leaders of the Wayanad Insurrection (Kottayam Pazhassi rebellion or [Kottayam War](/wiki/Cotiote_War "Cotiote War")). Pazhassi Raja was a member of the western branch of the Kottayam royal clan.[[2]](#cite_note-:0-2) When [Hyder Ali](/wiki/Hyder_Ali "Hyder Ali") of the [Kingdom of Mysore](/wiki/Kingdom_of_Mysore "Kingdom of Mysore") [occupied Malabar](/wiki/Mysore_invasion_of_Kerala "Mysore invasion of Kerala") in 1773, the Raja of Kottayam found political asylum in [Travancore](/wiki/Travancore "Travancore"). In 1790, the British recognized Pazhassi Raja as the head of Kottayam instead of the original Raja who had taken refuge at Travancore.[[3]](#cite_note-3)

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Kingdom_of_Kottayam&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

The origin of the Kottayam royal family is lost in obscurity. By tradition Harischandara Perumal who built a fort at Puralimala and resided there is regarded as the founder of the Kottayam family. The rajas of Kottayam were therefore called Puralisas and were also known as Purannattu rajas, who ruled over the land of Purainad. Descendants of the Puraikizhar family and are referred to in the Tirunelveli copper plate of Bhaskara Ravi Varman. At times, the part of Kolathunad, the Kottayam principality gradually acquired independent control over the territories lying in the [Tellichery](/wiki/Thalassery "Thalassery") taluk, shared the area with the Iruvazhinad Nambiars and also extended its jurisdiction up to the borders of [Coorg](/wiki/Coorg_Province "Coorg Province"). The family came to have three branches: Eastern, Southern and Western. The first two had their seat at Kottayam and the last at Pazhassi.[[2]](#cite_note-:0-2)

## Geography

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Kingdom_of_Kottayam&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Geography")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/4/4c/Wellington%2C_soldier_and_statesman%2C_and_the_revival_of_the_military_power_of_England_%281904%29_%2814593177119%29.jpg/250px-Wellington%2C_soldier_and_statesman%2C_and_the_revival_of_the_military_power_of_England_%281904%29_%2814593177119%29.jpg)](/wiki/File:Wellington,_soldier_and_statesman,_and_the_revival_of_the_military_power_of_England_(1904)_(14593177119).jpg)

India in 1804. [Thalassery](/wiki/Thalassery "Thalassery"), [Kozhikode](/wiki/Kozhikode "Kozhikode"), and [Kochi](/wiki/Kochi "Kochi") are marked as cities in the present-day state of Kerala

The Kingdom of Kottayam covered what is today Talassery taluk, [Iritty taluk](/wiki/Iritty_taluk "Iritty taluk") (1000 km2) of [Kannur District](/wiki/Kannur_District "Kannur District") and [Wayanad District](/wiki/Wayanad_District "Wayanad District") (2000 km2). The headquarters of this kingdom were located in [Kottayam](/wiki/Kottayam-Malabar "Kottayam-Malabar"), a small town not far from [Tellicherry](/wiki/Tellicherry "Tellicherry"). The royal [dynasty](/wiki/Dynasty "Dynasty") of the princely state of Kottayam was called Purannatt Swarupam. The Padinjare Kovilakam or Western Branch of this royal dynasty was located at Pazhassi and is famous for its heroic royal rebel, [Pazhassi Raja](/wiki/Pazhassi_Raja "Pazhassi Raja").

## Cradle of Kathakali

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Kingdom_of_Kottayam&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Cradle of Kathakali")]

The institution of [Kathakali](/wiki/Kathakali "Kathakali") gained in progress and richness during the time of the Raja of Kottayam, between 1665 AD and 1725 AD. The then-raja of Kottayam was a brilliant actor-dancer who structured several compositions to complete the transition of Kathakali from its earlier form, [Ramanattam](/wiki/Ramanattam "Ramanattam"), developed by Kottarakkara Thampuran. The Kottayam family produced two distinguished scholars, [Kerala Varma Thampuran](/wiki/Kerala_Varma_Valiya_Koil_Thampuran "Kerala Varma Valiya Koil Thampuran"), the author of *Valmiki Ramayanam Kilippattu* and Vidwan Thampuran, the great patron of Kathakali and composer of [Attakathas](/wiki/Aattakatha_(performance) "Aattakatha (performance)").[[2]](#cite_note-:0-2) Many dedicated artists like Chathu Panicker also endeavoured to lay the foundations for what is known as Kathakali now. Their efforts were concentrated on the rituals, classical details, and scriptural perfection. Even now the basic details of Kathakali have not changed considerably from this format. Bakavadham, Kirmeeravadham, Kalyana Saugandhikam, and Nivathakayacha Kalakeyavadham are the four perfect Kottayam works. After this, the most important changes in Kathakali were brought about through the efforts of a single person, namely [Kaplingad Narayanan Nambudiri](/w/index.php?title=Kaplingad_Narayanan_Nambudiri&action=edit&redlink=1 "Kaplingad Narayanan Nambudiri (page does not exist)"). After basic instruction in various faculties of the art in Vettathu Kalari of [North Malabar](/wiki/North_Malabar "North Malabar"), he shifted to Travancore and there in its capital and many other centres he found many willing to co-operate with him in bringing about these reforms.

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Kingdom_of_Kottayam&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: See also")]
